name: Foster Moreau
headline: Backup tight end in Houston with little fantasy juice
body:
The 2024 tape still tells the story of what Moreau can be in volume: 16 games, 69.1% snaps, 347 routes and a 43-target, 413-yard, five-touchdown line that paired a sturdy 1.19 yards per route with a sharp 0.463 EPA per play and 62.8% success rate. Nine red-zone looks and five end-zone targets made him a genuine scoring threat when the route share was real.

That role evaporated in 2025. Across seven games his snaps cratered to 35.7%, he ran just 94 routes, drew 10 targets and salvaged a 7-59-0 stat line with negative efficiency (minus-0.374 EPA per play). Now in Houston, he profiles as pure depth behind Dalton Schultz, landing around TE117 in consensus and rostered in essentially no leagues. Without an injury ahead of him, there's nothing here to chase.
